sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Left shoulder pain for the past three weeks.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports left shoulder pain beginning three weeks ago, initially triggered by activity, and now present with overhead movement and reaching. He denies prior shoulder pain and reports using acetaminophen with partial relief. He has not tried icing. He denies numbness or tingling in the arm or fingers. He has not had physical therapy before.
Findings
Physical examination reveals limited active and passive range of motion in the left shoulder and tenderness to palpation of the greater tuberosity. The left arm capillary refill is less than three seconds, and sensation is intact to light touch. The x-ray of the shoulder was normal.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy.
Plan
I am referring the patient to physical therapy for approximately six to eight weeks to strengthen the shoulder muscles. The patient can continue taking acetaminophen as needed. If symptoms do not improve, a steroid injection may be considered.
